content: Diagnostic evaluation: Morning (8AM) serum cortisol - <3 mcg/dL diagnostic of AI, >18 mcg/dL excludes AI, intermediate requires stimulation testing. ACTH level distinguishes primary (elevated) from secondary/tertiary (low/normal). Cosyntropin (ACTH) stimulation test: gold standard - administer 250mcg cosyntropin IM/IV, measure cortisol at 0, 30, 60 minutes; normal response peak cortisol ≥18-20 mcg/dL. Low-dose (1mcg) test more sensitive for secondary AI. Plasma renin activity elevated in primary AI (mineralocorticoid deficiency). Additional workup for primary AI: 21-hydroxylase antibodies (autoimmune), very-long-chain fatty acids (adrenoleukodystrophy), adrenal CT (hemorrhage, infiltration, TB calcification). For secondary/tertiary: pituitary MRI, other pituitary hormone assessment. Consider insulin tolerance test or metyrapone test if cosyntropin test equivocal for central AI.
